summary: Tightened qd report required text-field validation so whitespace-only strings are rejected after trimming, while keeping the existing file-scoped report_error and expect_jq validation pattern.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: scripts/qd-reports-check.sh uses a reusable jq predicate for required non-blank report text fields.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: scripts/qd-reports-check.sh defines a shared nonblank predicate through expect_jq, trimming leading and trailing whitespace before checking string length.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Completion report checks reject blank nodeId, summary, changedFiles[], nested acceptance evidence strings, nested command strings, evidence[], and realWorldValidation.evidence.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: scripts/qd-reports-check.sh applies nonblank to the requested completion fields; evidence/m4-qd-report-nonblank-content-contract/negative-smoke-whitespace-summary.log confirms a whitespace-only summary is rejected.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Audit report checks reject blank nodeId, nested acceptanceReviewed strings, and realWorldValidation.evidence.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: scripts/qd-reports-check.sh applies nonblank to the requested audit fields while preserving qd-compatible verificationEvidence boolean validation.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Linux validation evidence records the negative smoke test, qd report validation, full check, and whitespace diff check.
Existing qd reports pass the stricter check.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-qd-report-nonblank-content-contract/commands.log records `nix develop -c just qd-reports-check` passing with this node's reports included.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: No macOS-specific validation is claimed or required.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: realWorldValidation.required is false because this is repository qd report metadata validation only.

commandsRun.command: temporary-copy negative smoke test with completion summary = "   \n\t"

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node changes repository qd report metadata validation only; no product runtime or hardware validation is required.
